APSIC Environmental Hygiene Excellence Award

Be recognised.
Save lives.

Identifying, recognising and celebrating hospitals across Asia Pacific that demonstrate excellence in environmental hygiene and patient safety.

Register Your Interest

About the Award

The APSIC Environmental Hygiene Excellence Award is an invaluable recognition for healthcare facilities across Asia Pacific.

The APSIC Environmental Hygiene Excellence Award (AEHEA) has been established as a regional platform to identify, recognise, honour, celebrate, and share best practices in environmental hygiene that demonstrate excellence in championing and sustaining the highest standard of hygiene and safety.

We would encourage nominees that have meaningfully contributed to this region.

Register Our Award Contact

Commitment to IPC

Hospitals demonstrate unwavering dedication to infection prevention and control practices across all departments.

Innovative practices

Leading institutions implement novel evidence-based interventions that advance hygiene standards and outcomes.

Quality of care

Excellence in environmental hygiene directly translates to measurable improvements in patient safety and clinical outcomes.

Leadership Message

A message from our Award Chair & APSIC President

The APSIC Environmental Hygiene Excellence Award celebrates healthcare institutions that demonstrate exceptional leadership and measurable success in maintaining safe environments. Hygiene is a critical step toward safeguarding patient lives.

One of the core components in the infection prevention and control programme is appropriate environmental hygiene practices and protocols. These facilitate practices related to the prevention and control of healthcare associated infections. It is the responsibility of healthcare institutions to ensure a safe physical hygiene environment.

To pursue these objectives, we would like to invite you to participate in the APSIC Environmental Hygiene Excellence Award and be part of a region-wide movement to elevate care standards across Asia Pacific.

A/Prof. Moi Lin Ling
Program Chair, AEHEA
APSIC Environmental Hygiene Excellence Award
Prof. Anucha Apisarnthanarak
President, APSIC
Asia Pacific Society of Clinical Microbiology and Infection

Why Join

Why Join the APSIC Environmental Hygiene Excellence Award?

Be Recognised

Showcase your institution's leadership and excellence in environment hygiene across the Asia Pacific region.

Save Lives

Implementing environment hygiene strategies significantly reduces patient morbidity and mortality.

Regional Stage

Celebrated on the regional stage

Present Your Programme

Selected hospitals are invited to share best practices via a local presentation at the APSIC Congress — putting your institution on the regional stage.

Register Your Interest

Share With the Region

Representatives exchange their experiences, innovations, and activities with the regional infection prevention community at APSIC Congress.

Register Your Interest

Recognition

Prestigious recognition, rather than a monetary prize

Finalist institutions are honoured with a visit from an independent panel of APSIC experts, and leave with constructive feedback and expert insight that strengthens their programmes long after the award.

▶ Continuous improvement

Expert guidance to refine and advance your environmental hygiene practices.

▶ Sustainability of programmes

Support to embed lasting change, not one-off wins.

▶ Outcomes & impact

Help to measure and demonstrate your contribution to patient safety.

FAQ

Your questions, answered

Everything you need to know about eligibility, the process, and what you'll gain from participating in the AEHEA programme.

Can't find the answer you're looking for?

Contact Us
Hospitals and healthcare institutions across the Asia Pacific region are eligible to apply. Participation is open to institutions at various stages of environmental hygiene development.
The programme aims to acknowledge excellence in environmental hygiene, encourage participation and evaluation, and provide a platform for benchmarking and recognition.
Participation in the Award Programme is not associated with a monetary prize or fee. Any specific requirements will be communicated during the registration phase.
Submissions are assessed on commitment to infection prevention and environmental hygiene practices, innovation and sustainability, and impact on patient safety.
The APSIC Expert Panel reviews all submitted applications using a structured scoring system and shortlists institutions that demonstrate excellence and strong potential.
Participants gain a structured validation framework, insights from regional experts, recognition in the professional community, and motivation to further environmental hygiene excellence.
Yes. All submissions are handled with strict confidentiality and used solely for evaluation purposes within the programme.
